labels.notes: Gary Phil Stewart (seen with son Malaki Stewart) — UI screenshot shows session in History as 'Auto-charted #4 — Gary Phil Stewart, 02:08 PM, 31:15 duration, 2845 words' with SOAP tab green-checkmark but EMPTY content; Generate-SOAP button does not work. NO LOCAL ARCHIVE DIRECTORY MATCHES this session on Room 6: today's local archive has 9 dirs + 2 partial orphans, none with patient_name='Gary' or word_count near 2845. The screenshot timestamp (14:08 EDT, 31:15 dur, 2845 words) does not match any encounter_split event in day_log.jsonl for either continuous-mode run. DEFECT (Class G — server-side metadata + missing local file): hypothesis is the session lives on the profile-service (recorded by another physical machine — Room 2 iMac or process_mobile from iOS) and was synced into the History list via the hybrid-history merge in commands/archive.rs. The has_soap_note flag from server metadata renders the green checkmark, but get_session_soap_note reads local FIRST and returns empty when the local soap_note.txt is missing. Generate-SOAP button calls save_local_soap_note which validates the session_id then writes to the local archive dir — likely fails because either (a) no local dir was created when the session was first listed, or (b) the date-path resolution for cross-machine sessions is wrong. CANNOT VERIFY SESSION_ID without profile-service access (Tailscale connection to 100.119.83.76 timed out at 75s during this review). Action items: (1) make get_session_soap_note fall back to server-side soap_note when local is empty AND server says has_soap_note=true; (2) ensure the History row click triggers a server-fetch + local-write of soap_note.txt + transcript.txt + metadata.json so subsequent regen has a real local dir to work with; (3) make Generate-SOAP fail loudly with a useful error when the session has no transcript locally.
